title: Four stroke internal combustion engine of v-twin layout with innovative arrangement
abstract: This invention is a four-stroke internal combustion engine of V-twin layout with innovative arrangements, primarily meant for motorcycles. Innovations basically comprising; a) clutch placed on crankshaft, b) two phase main power take off and some of its gears placed on output shaft of transmission, c) transmission with innovative 'bypass drive' feature, d) crankshaft assembly with innovative geometry, e) innovative integrated trigger wheel, f) dog-clutch starter drive engagement, g) alternator repositioned off crankshaft, h) split rocker-arm pivots, i) airboxes positioned between cylinders and using snorkel intakes, j) lubricant pump using crankcase as part of housing, k) tier-wise accommodation of spark-plugs and block fasteners, l) lubricant connections to/from oil-cooler made through front of crankcase.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: CLAIM 1 : An internal combustion engine (43) comprising; a clutch (3), wherein said clutch (3) is mounted on the crankshaft (13), and wherein torque is transmitted from the said clutch (3) to the input shaft (10) of a transmission utilizing a plurality of gears (4)(5)(6)(7)(8), and said plurality of gears reduce speed and increase torque in two stages, and wherein amongst said plurality of gears (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) two gears (6)(7) are mounted on the output shaft (9) of said transmission but are free to rotate independent of said output shaft (9), and wherein amongst said two gears (6)(7) which are mounted on said output shaft (9), a first gear (6) through means of a dog clutch meshes with a component (1 1 ) that is mounted on said output shaft (9) and torque is transmitted from said first gear (6) to said output shaft (9) through means of said component (1 1 ), said component (1 1 ) being rotationally fixed to said output shaft (9) but being free to move to a degree in relation to the axis of said output shaft (9); a crankshaft, wherein said crankshaft contains at least one crank web (2) which has a circular outer profile, said crank web (2) possessing on a first face (20) a first depression (22), and on a second face (21 ) a second depression (23), wherein the mass of the absent material owing to said first depression (22) and the mass of the absent material owing to said second depression (23) solely account for the counterweight characteristic of said crank web (2); a first component (24) which is utilized by an electronic sensor to sense rotational position and rotational speed of a crankshaft (14), and wherein said first component (24) also possesses dogs (25) on a face, wherein said dogs (25) engage with dogs of another component (29) to transmit or receive torque, and wherein said another component (29) belongs to a drive mechanism, said drive mechanism being utilized to transmit torque from an electrical motor to said crankshaft (14) in order to start said internal combustion engine; an alternator (28), wherein the rotating element of said alternator is mounted on a shaft (32), the axis of rotation of said shaft (32) being parallel to the axis of rotation of a crankshaft (14); 

 at least one valvetrain, wherein said valvetrain is comprised of a plurality of rocker arms (34)(35) mounted on two pivots (36)(37), wherein amongst said two pivots (36)(37) one pivot (36) being parted into two segments, said two segments possessing the same axis, and between said two segments existing a gap, and wherein at least one rocker arm (35) occupies any amount of space of such said gap either when said rocker arm (35) is static, or during the motions of said rocker arm (35); two engine blocks (39)(40), wherein each engine block contains at least one cylinder, wherein there exists a throttle assembly (45) between said engine blocks (39)(40), and there exist two airboxes (41 ) mounted directly onto said throttle assembly (45) on two different sides, wherein said throttle assembly possesses openings on said two different sides to draw in air from said airboxes (41 ), and wherein said airboxes (41 ) draw in air through vestibules (42), said vestibules reaching to above one or more of said engine blocks (39); a mechanical pump to pump lubricant, wherein said mechanical pump is affixed to a predetermined location of the crankcase of said internal combustion engine, wherein said predetermined location of the crankcase contains one or more of said lubricant pumpâs bearings (53), and wherein said lubricant pump feeds lubricant directly into an oil passage contained in said crankcase without the use of a hose or pipe or the like; a fastener (55) to fasten an engine block (39) onto said internal combustion engineâs crankcase (38), wherein the space required to access or remove said fastener (55) coincides with the space required to access or assemble or remove a spark plug; one or more predetermined locations (58)(59) on the crankcase (38), where hoses or ducts or pipes connect to, said hoses or ducts or pipes leading to or from a radiator or oil cooler or heat exchanger, said predetermined locations (58)(59) being disposed on a side of said crankcase, amongst all sides of the crankcase said side being closest to the front of the motorcycle that said internal combustion engine is mounted on. 

 CLAIM 2: An internal combustion engine (43), characterized by the clutch (3) being mounted on the crankshaft (13); and wherein torque is transmitted from the said clutch (3) to the input shaft (10) of a transmission utilizing a plurality of gears (4)(5)(6)(7)(8); and said plurality of gears reduce speed and increase torque in two stages; and wherein amongst said plurality of gears (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) two gears (6)(7) are mounted on the output shaft (9) of said transmission but are free to rotate independent of said output shaft (9). CLAIM 3: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 2, and wherein said two gears (6)(7) which are mounted on said output shaft (9) are linked to each other through means of a dog clutch. CLAIM 4: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 2 or Claim 3, and wherein amongst said two gears (6)(7) which are mounted on said output shaft (9), a first gear (6) through means of a dog clutch meshes with a component (1 1 ) that is mounted on said output shaft (9) and torque is transmitted from said first gear (6) to said output shaft (9) through means of said component (1 1 ); said component (1 1 ) being rotationally fixed to said output shaft (9) but being free to move to a degree in relation to the axis of said output shaft (9). CLAIM 5: An internal combustion engine (43) that is characterized by the clutch (3) being mounted on the crankshaft (13), and wherein said internal combustion engine is of V-twin type, wherein said internal combustion engine possesses two cylinders; said two cylinders forming an angle; said two cylinders come closer together near the crankshaft; and said two cylinders extend away from each other on the side of said cylinders with the cylinder head. 

 CLAIM 6: An internal combustion engine (43) that is characterized by the clutch (3) mounted on the crankshaft; wherein said internal combustion engine possesses two cylinders or less. CLAIM 7: A mechanical device (43) characterized by a group of components (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) that are utilized to transmit torque originating from a prime mover to the input shaft (10) of a transmission; wherein amongst said group of components (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) one or more components (6)(7) are mounted on the output shaft (9) of said transmission but are free to rotate independent of said output shaft (9). CLAIM 8: A mechanical device as claimed in Claim 7, and wherein said group of components (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) reduce speed and increase torque in two stages. CLAIM 9: A mechanical device as claimed in Claim 7 or Claim 8, and wherein said mechanical device is utilized to power an automobile. CLAIM 10: A mechanical device as claimed in Claim 7 or Claim 8, and wherein said mechanical device is an internal combustion engine. CLAIM 11 : A mechanical device as claimed in Claim 7 or Claim 8, and wherein said mechanical device is an internal combustion engine that is of V-twin type; wherein said internal combustion engine possesses two cylinders; said two cylinders forming an angle; said two cylinders come closer together near the crankshaft; and said two cylinders extend away from each other on the side of said cylinders with the cylinder head. 

 CLAIM 12: A mechanical device (43) comprising a first component (1 1 ) which is mounted on the output shaft (9) of a transmission, wherein said first component (11 ) is rotationally fixed to said output shaft (9) but is free to move to a degree along the axis of said output shaft (9); and said mechanical device also comprises a second component (6) which is mounted on said output shaft (9), wherein said second component (6) is axially locked in place, but is enabled to rotate independent of said output shaft (9); and said first component (1 1 ) meshes with said second component (6) through means of a dog clutch or the like; through such meshing torque is transmitted from said second component (6) to said output shaft (9) through means of said first component (1 1 ); wherein said second component (6) is part of a group of components (4)(5)(6)(7)(8) that are utilized to transmit torque originating from a prime mover to the input shaft (10) of said transmission. CLAIM 13: An internal combustion engine (43) that comprises at least one crankshaft; wherein said crankshaft contains at least one crank web (2) which has a circular outer profile; said crank web (2) possessing on a first face (20) a first depression (22), and on a second face (21 ) a second depression (23); wherein the mass of the absent material owing to said first depression (22) and the mass of the absent material owing to said second depression (23) solely account for the counterweight characteristic of said crank web (2). CLAIM 14: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 13, and wherein a fastening medium (19) for affixing a shaft (14) onto said crank web (2) is sunk into said first depression (22), and a fastening medium (18) for affixing a crankpin (15) onto said crank web (2) is sunk into said second depression (23). CLAIM 15: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 13 or Claim 14, and wherein said crankshaft possesses two such said crank webs (1 )(2). 

 CLAIM 16: An internal combustion engine (43) comprising a first component (24) which is utilized by an electronic sensor to sense rotational position and rotational speed of a crankshaft (14); and wherein said first component (24) also possesses dogs (25) on a face; wherein said dogs (25) engage with dogs of another component (29) to transmit torque. CLAIM 17: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 16, and wherein said another component (29) belongs to a drive mechanism; said drive mechanism being utilized to transmit torque from an electrical motor to said crankshaft (14). CLAIM 18: An internal combustion engine (43) that comprises an alternator (28); wherein the rotating element of said alternator is mounted on a shaft (32); the axis of rotation of said shaft (32) being parallel to the axis of rotation of a crankshaft (14); wherein said internal combustion engine powers a motorcycle. CLAIM 19: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 18, and wherein said internal combustion engine is of V-twin type; wherein said internal combustion engine contains two cylinders; said two cylinders forming an angle; said two cylinders come closer together near the crankshaft; and said two cylinders extend away from each other on the side of said cylinders with the cylinder head. CLAIM 20: An internal combustion engine (43) comprising an electrical motor utilized to start said internal combustion engine; wherein a drive mechanism is utilized to transmit torque from said electrical motor to a crankshaft (14) of said internal combustion engine; wherein a first component (29) is mounted on said crankshaft, but is free to rotate independent of said crankshaft (14); said first component being part of said drive mechanism; said first component (29) transmits torque through means 

 of a dog clutch to a second component (24) which is also mounted on said crankshaft (14); said second component being rotationally fixed to said crankshaft (14); and said second component (24) transmits the torque it receives from said first component (29) to said crankshaft (14). CLAIM 21 : An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 20, and wherein said first component (29) is free to move to a degree along the axis of said crankshaft (14); the engagement of said dog clutch being actuated by one or more solenoids (30). CLAIM 22: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 20 or Claim 21 , and wherein said second component (24) is also utilized by an electrical sensor to sense rotational position and rotational speed of said crankshaft (14). CLAIM 23: An internal combustion engine (43) comprising at least one valvetrain; wherein said valvetrain contains a plurality of rocker arms (34)(35) mounted on two pivots (36)(37); wherein amongst said two pivots (36)(37) one pivot (36) being parted into two segments; said two segments possessing the same axis; and between said two segments existing a gap; and wherein at least one rocker arm (35) occupies any amount of space of such said gap either when said rocker arm (35) is static, or during the motions of said rocker arm (35). CLAIM 24: An internal combustion engine (43) comprising two engine blocks (39)(40) and a throttle assembly; and wherein each engine block contains at least one cylinder; wherein said throttle assembly (45) is positioned between said engine blocks (39)(40); and there exist two airboxes (41 ) mounted directly onto said throttle assembly (45) on two different sides; wherein said throttle assembly possesses openings on two different sides to draw in air from said airboxes (41 ). 

 CLAIM 25: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 24, and wherein said airboxes (41 ) draw in air through vestibules (42); said vestibules reaching to above one or more of said engine blocks (39). CLAIM 26: An internal combustion engine comprising a mechanical pump to pump lubricant; wherein said mechanical pump is affixed to a predetermined location of the crankcase of said internal combustion engine; wherein said predetermined location of the crankcase contains one or more of said lubricant pumpâs bearings (53). CLAIM 27: An internal combustion engine as claimed in Claim 26, and wherein said lubricant pump feeds lubricant directly into an oil passage contained in said crankcase without the use of a hose or pipe or the like. CLAIM 28: An internal combustion engine comprising at least in one location, a fastener (55) to fasten an engine block (39) onto said internal combustion engineâs crankcase (38); wherein the space required to access or remove said fastener (55) coincides with the space required to access or assemble or remove a spark plug. CLAIM 29: An internal combustion engine (43) comprising one or more predetermined locations (58)(59) on the crankcase (38) where hoses or ducts or pipes connect to; said hoses or ducts or pipes leading to or from a radiator or oil cooler or heat exchanger; said predetermined locations (58)(59) being disposed on a side of said crankcase; said side being foremost amongst the sides of said crankcase, when a motorcycle that said internal combustion engine would be mounted on moves forward; and wherein said internal combustion engine is of V-twin type; wherein said internal combustion engine possesses two cylinders; said two cylinders forming an angle; said two cylinders come closer together near the crankshaft; and 

 said two cylinders extend away from each other on the side of said cylinders with the cylinder head.
